annotations.label: p-group

anchor.snippet: a p -group is a group whose cardinality is a power of

annotations.note: `IsPGroup p G` is defined as every element having p-power order, equivalent to `Nat.card G = p^n` for finite G (see `IsPGroup.iff_card`).

annotations.label: Sylow p-subgroup

anchor.snippet: A Sylow p -subgroup (sometimes p -Sylow subgroup ) of a finite group

annotations.note: The `Sylow p G` structure bundles a maximal p-subgroup of `G`.

annotations.label: Sylow theorems (lead summary)

anchor.snippet: The Sylow theorems state that for every prime factor

annotations.note: All three Sylow theorems are proved in `Mathlib.GroupTheory.Sylow` via `Sylow.nonempty`, `Sylow.isPretransitive_of_finite`, and `card_sylow_modEq_one`.

annotations.label: Order of Sylow p-subgroups

anchor.snippet: all members are actually isomorphic to each other and have the largest possible order

annotations.note: `Sylow.card_eq_multiplicity` shows `Nat.card P = p ^ Nat.factorization (Nat.card G) p`, giving the maximal p-power order.

annotations.label: Sylow Theorem 1 (Existence)
annotations.note: `Sylow.nonempty` (with `Sylow.card_eq_multiplicity`) gives the existence of a Sylow p-subgroup of order p^n for n the p-multiplicity of |G|.

annotations.label: Cauchy's theorem (Corollary)
annotations.note: `exists_prime_orderOf_dvd_card` is Cauchy's theorem: a prime dividing |G| yields an element of that order.

annotations.label: Sylow Theorem 2 (Conjugacy)
annotations.note: Together with `IsPGroup.exists_le_sylow`, the transitive G-action on `Sylow p G` (`Sylow.isPretransitive_of_finite`) gives Sylow II.

annotations.label: Sylow Theorem 3 (Number of Sylow subgroups)
annotations.note: `card_sylow_modEq_one` proves n_p ≡ 1 (mod p), with `Sylow.card_dvd_index` giving n_p ∣ [G:P].

annotations.label: Subgroup of every prime-power order

anchor.snippet: then there exists a subgroup of

annotations.note: `exists_subgroup_card_pow_prime` produces a subgroup of order p^n whenever p^n ∣ |G|.

annotations.label: Sylow subgroups have equal order and are conjugate

anchor.snippet: The Sylow theorems imply that for a prime number

annotations.note: `Sylow.isPretransitive_of_finite` says G acts transitively on Sylow p-subgroups; `Sylow.equiv` provides the resulting isomorphism.

annotations.label: Normality of unique Sylow subgroup

anchor.snippet: An important consequence of Theorem 2

annotations.note: `Sylow.normal_of_subsingleton` (with `Sylow.unique_of_normal`) shows a unique Sylow subgroup is normal.

annotations.label: n_p divides |G|

anchor.snippet: The third bullet point of the third theorem has as an immediate consequence

annotations.note: `Sylow.card_dvd_index` gives `Nat.card (Sylow p G) ∣ P.index`, the stronger form implying n_p ∣ |G|.

annotations.label: Sylow p-subgroup (infinite group)

anchor.snippet: One defines a Sylow p -subgroup in an infinite group to be a p -subgroup

annotations.note: The `Sylow` structure is defined for arbitrary groups as a maximal p-subgroup, with no finiteness assumption.

annotations.label: Sylow theorem for infinite groups
annotations.note: `IsPGroup.exists_le_sylow` shows every p-subgroup is contained in a Sylow p-subgroup, proved via Zorn without finiteness.

annotations.label: Sylow subgroups of dihedral group (n odd)

anchor.snippet: A simple illustration of Sylow subgroups and the Sylow theorems are the dihedral group

annotations.note: Mathlib has `DihedralGroup` but no specific computation of its Sylow subgroups.

annotations.label: Sylow 2-subgroups of dihedral group (n even)

anchor.snippet: By contrast, if n is even, then 4 divides the order of the group

annotations.note: No file in `Mathlib/GroupTheory/SpecificGroups/Dihedral.lean` mentions Sylow.

annotations.label: Sylow p-subgroups of GL_2(F_q)

anchor.snippet: Another example are the Sylow p-subgroups of GL

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `GeneralLinearGroup` but does not identify its Sylow subgroups.

annotations.label: Diagonal matrix Sylow subgroup

anchor.snippet: One such subgroup P , is the set of diagonal matrices

annotations.note: No Mathlib formalization of upper-triangular Sylow subgroups of GL_n(F_q).

annotations.label: Burnside's p^a q^b theorem

anchor.snippet: Burnside's p a q b theorem states that if the order of a group is the product of one or two prime powers

annotations.note: Mathlib has Burnside's transfer/fusion theorems but not the p^a q^b solvability theorem.

annotations.label: Groups of order 15 are cyclic

anchor.snippet: Let G be a group of order 15 = 3 · 5

annotations.note: No specific Mathlib lemma asserts groups of order 15 are cyclic.

annotations.label: Groups of order pq are cyclic

anchor.snippet: if n = pq , where p and q are distinct prime numbers such that p does not divide q-1 , then every group of order n is cyclic

annotations.note: Mathlib has Sylow infrastructure but no generic `isCyclic_of_card_eq_pq` lemma.

annotations.label: No simple group of order 30

anchor.snippet: If | G | = 30 and G were simple

annotations.note: No Mathlib statement that no simple group of order 30 exists.

annotations.label: No simple group of order 42

anchor.snippet: Next suppose | G | = 42 = 2 · 3 · 7 and G were simple

annotations.note: No Mathlib statement about non-existence of simple groups of order 42.

annotations.label: Smallest non-cyclic simple group is A_5

anchor.snippet: the smallest simple non-cyclic group is A

annotations.note: `alternatingGroup.isSimpleGroup` proves A_n is simple for n ≥ 5, but minimality of A_5 among non-cyclic simple groups is not stated.

annotations.label: Wilson's theorem

anchor.snippet: Part of Wilson's theorem states that

annotations.note: `Nat.prime_iff_fac_equiv_neg_one` states `(n-1)! ≡ -1 (mod n) ↔ n prime` for `n ≠ 1`.

annotations.label: Burnside's fusion theorem

anchor.snippet: a slight generalization known as Burnside's fusion theorem states that if G is a finite group with Sylow

annotations.note: `Sylow.conj_eq_normalizer_conj_of_mem` proves the fusion property for elements in a commutative Sylow subgroup, a special case of the general fusion theorem; the focal subgroup theorem is also formalized in `Mathlib.GroupTheory.Focal`.

annotations.label: p-nilpotency from central Sylow normalizer

anchor.snippet: if G is a finite group whose Sylow p -subgroup P is contained in the center of its normalizer

annotations.note: `ker_transferSylow_isComplement'` is Burnside's normal p-complement theorem: if N(P) ≤ C(P) then P has a normal complement.

annotations.label: Theorem 1 (proof statement)
annotations.note: Documented in the file as 'Sylow's first theorem', producing a subgroup of order p^n when p^n ∣ |G|.

annotations.label: Fixed-points lemma
annotations.note: `IsPGroup.card_modEq_card_fixedPoints` says |X| ≡ |X^G| (mod p) for a finite p-group G acting on X.

annotations.label: Theorem 2 (proof statement)
annotations.note: `Sylow.isPretransitive_of_finite` formalizes the conjugacy of Sylow p-subgroups.

annotations.label: Theorem 3 (proof statement)
annotations.note: `card_sylow_modEq_one` and `Sylow.card_dvd_index` together give Sylow III.

annotations.label: Constructive existence of Sylow p-subgroup

anchor.snippet: if H is a p -subgroup of G and the index

annotations.note: `exists_subgroup_card_pow_succ` produces K ⊇ H of order p^(n+1) when p^(n+1) ∣ |G| and |H| = p^n.

annotations.label: Polynomial-time Sylow algorithm for permutation groups

anchor.snippet: it has been proven, in Kantor

annotations.note: Algorithmic complexity results for computing Sylow subgroups are outside Mathlib's scope.
